food_subgroup: Fast food beef patty meal with rice and gravy

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for Jollibee Burger Steak specifically; closest_parent 'beef patty food product' (FOODON_00002372) used as the nearest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Beef burger patty) is the closest parent; no specific code exists for a complete burger steak meal with rice and gravy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT food concept exists for this specific fast-food meal combination.

serving.description: 1 meal (2 beef patties, rice, mushroom gravy)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 450 g, comprising two beef burger steak patties served over steamed white rice and topped with mushroom gravy, as typically plated at Jollibee Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Beef patty, cooked; White rice, cooked; Gravy, beef or mushroom — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods; supplemented with Jollibee Philippines published nutrition information for Burger Steak
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Jollibee Foods Corporation (Philippines) published nutrition data
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No Singapore-specific HPB Foodo entry exists for Jollibee Burger Steak Meal. Nutrient values are estimated by component assembly: (1) USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for cooked ground beef patty (FDC ID 174032), (2) USDA SR Legacy cooked white rice (FDC ID 169756), (3) USDA SR Legacy beef/mushroom gravy (FDC ID 172462), weighted to approximate a 450 g meal (2 patties ~160 g total, rice ~200 g, gravy ~90 g). Values are per 100 g of the assembled meal. Jollibee Philippines published nutrition panel used as a cross-check for plausibility.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — white rice (boiled) GI ~64–72; mixed beef and rice meal estimated GI ~55–60
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K et al. (2021) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values 2021. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Jollibee Burger Steak Meal. GI estimated at 56 based on the dominant carbohydrate source being steamed white rice (GI ~64 per University of Sydney GI Database for boiled white rice) attenuated by the substantial protein (~9.2 g/100 g) and fat (~5.8 g/100 g) content of the beef patties and gravy, which are known to lower the effective GI of mixed meals. Analogous mixed rice-and-meat meal GI values from the University of Sydney GI Database (range 50–60) support this estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (56 × 91.8 g carbohydrate in 450 g serving) / 100 ≈ 51.5.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~310 mg/100 g; ~1395 mg per full meal serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal due to its high sodium load (~1395 mg per serving), which represents approximately 60% of the WHO-recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg; portion reduction or requesting reduced-sodium gravy is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~51.5 per full serving) driven by white rice as the primary carbohydrate source
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with this meal due to its high glycemic load per serving (~51.5) from white rice; consuming a smaller portion, substituting brown rice if available, and pairing with non-starchy vegetables can help moderate postprandial blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~2.1 g/100 g; ~9.5 g per serving) and sodium content from processed beef patties and gravy
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this meal due to its saturated fat (~9.5 g per serving) and high sodium (~1395 mg per serving), both of which are associated with elevated LDL cholesterol and blood pressure; occasional consumption within an otherwise heart-healthy dietary pattern is preferable to regular intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The 2-Piece Burger Steak Meal is a popular fast-food dish served at Jollibee, a Filipino-origin quick-service restaurant chain with outlets across Singapore and Southeast Asia. The meal consists of two seasoned ground beef patties that are grilled or pan-fried and served over a bed of steamed white rice, generously topped with a savoury mushroom-based brown gravy. The beef patties are compact, mildly seasoned, and have a firm yet juicy texture, while the mushroom gravy adds umami depth and moisture to the dish. The combination of rice and beef patties makes this a filling, energy-dense meal. Nutritionally, the dish provides a moderate amount of protein (~9.2 g/100 g) from the beef, a significant carbohydrate load from the white rice (~20.4 g/100 g), and moderate fat (~5.8 g/100 g) including saturated fat from the beef. Sodium content is notably high (~310 mg/100 g; ~1395 mg per full serving), making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic load per serving is high (~51.5) due to the white rice component, which is relevant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The meal is tagged as Halal-certified at Jollibee Singapore. It is a comfort food staple that appeals to diners seeking a hearty, affordable, and familiar fast-food experience in Singapore.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Jollibee Burger Steak Meal?
Is Jollibee Burger Steak Halal in Singapore?
What is in the Jollibee Burger Steak Meal?
Sodium content of Jollibee Burger Steak
Can diabetics eat Jollibee Burger Steak?

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN entry exists for Jollibee Burger Steak Meal. Nutrient values are estimated by component assembly using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for cooked ground beef patty, cooked white rice, and mushroom/beef gravy, weighted to the approximate meal composition. Jollibee Philippines published nutrition panel was used as a plausibility cross-check. GI is an analogous estimate based on the dominant white-rice carbohydrate source attenuated by protein and fat. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported in the USDA SR Legacy component entries used for this assembled meal estimate. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary USDA SR Legacy sources used. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported across all component sources used in the assembly estimate. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol, vitamins A/B1/B2/B3/B6/B12, folate, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, and water are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data and should be treated as approximate. The meal is served at Jollibee Bugis Village, Singapore, and is tagged Halal per field survey data listing. Price SGD 8.80 per field survey data source record. The '(D)' suffix in the original food name likely denotes a meal variant designation (e.g., drink included or specific combo code) used by the restaurant or delivery platform; this has been normalised to '2-Piece Burger Steak Meal' as the canonical English name. Serving size of 450 g is an estimate based on typical Jollibee Burger Steak Meal plating; exact weight may vary. All -1 sentinel values reflect genuine absence of data in the assembled source evidence, not zero values.
